commands.review_summary:
The PR changes agent web_search runtime-context resolution so configured search providers keep runtime-provider discovery enabled while web_fetch keeps bundled-owner gating, with focused regression test updates.

PR surface: Source -4, Tests -22. Total -26 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run in this read-only review. The source path is clear on current main, and the linked report provides a long-running Brave gateway reproduction, but the failing gateway state was not recreated here.

target.title: Keep agent web_search on runtime provider resolution
target.body:
Fixes #87347

## User-facing bug
Agent-side `web_search` could report `web_search is disabled or no provider is available` even when the Brave web search provider was already loaded in the runtime and worked through `openclaw infer web`. The agent tool was deciding that a manifest-owned configured provider should avoid runtime provider discovery before `runWebSearch` had a chance to use the active runtime registry.

## Exact repro
Source-level reproduction:

1. Configure `tools.web.search.provider` to a manifest-owned provider such as `brave`.
2. Start a runtime where the Brave web search provider is loaded and visible through runtime/plugin inspection.
3. Invoke the agent-side `web_search` tool with no active runtime metadata on the tool call.
4. Before this fix, `resolveWebSearchToolRuntimeContext()` consulted manifest ownership and passed `preferRuntimeProviders: false` to `runWebSearch`, so execution could diverge from the loaded runtime provider view.
5. After this fix, agent-side `web_search` keeps `preferRuntimeProviders: true`; `runWebSearch` still applies manifest ownership as a runtime load-scope hint, but it loads from the live runtime registry first.

## Fix summary
- Kept agent-side `web_search` runtime-provider discovery enabled regardless of configured provider manifest ownership.
- Left `web_fetch` behavior unchanged: bundled fetch owners still disable runtime provider discovery where that path expects static/bundled provider loading.
- Updated the late-bind and runtime-context tests so manifest-owned configured search providers now prove the runtime-first path instead of the old static-provider preference.

## Targeted tests
- Updated `src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts` to assert search never resolves manifest owners at the agent-tool preference gate, while fetch still does.
- Updated `src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts` to assert configured manifest-owned search providers pass `preferRuntimeProviders: true` into `runWebSearch`.
- Re-ran the existing `web_tools` defaults tests and focused `web-search/runtime` provider-loading tests to cover the downstream runtime behavior.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Agent-side `web_search` now uses the same live runtime provider path as `runWebSearch` / `openclaw infer web`, instead of switching configured manifest-owned providers to static provider loading at the agent-tool layer.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/agent-web-search-provider-resolution`, commit `1c171b7e`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.ts src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts -t "web tool runtime context|web_search late-bound runtime fallback|web tools defaults|scopes runtime provider loading|honors preferRuntimeProviders|keeps runtime provider loading"`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Targeted Vitest passed: `Test Files 7 passed (7)`, `Tests 40 passed | 23 skipped (63)`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no actionable regressions.

Observed result after fix: Manifest-owned configured search providers keep runtime discovery enabled at the agent tool boundary; the runtime path remains responsible for provider scoping.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` did not reach changed-file checks. In the sandbox it failed during `pnpm install` with npm registry DNS `ENOTFOUND`. An escalated retry got past DNS but still failed during `pnpm install` when Darwin optional package downloads timed out with error `23`.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No live Brave API request was run from this environment; validation is source-level with targeted runtime/tool tests.
- No live Brave-backed web search request was run from a real agent conversation; coverage is through provider-resolution tests.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level reproduction, targeted regression tests, and `codex review --base origin/main` to validate the behavior above.
